hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) while reducing off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5 via steric differentiation.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong binding (ddG) but may not be sufficiently diverse or optimized for selectivity. Focusing on disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions is critical to push Δmargin further above 0.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) that are likely enabling off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
A low number of candidates reduces the chances of finding high-quality, selective designs. Increasing the number of candidates will improve diversity and coverage of the design space.
